hero.headline: Amity Online MBA in Finance and Accounting Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. NAAC A+, NIRF #22. QS ranked. WES recognised.

sections.tldr: Amity University Online MBA in Finance and Accounting Management suits working professionals targeting CFO-track, investment banking, or corporate finance roles. NAAC A+, NIRF #22 university credential at Rs 1,99,000. Sem 3-4 spec syllabus is confirmed each admission cycle — our counsellor shares it within 24 hours.

about.body:
Finance and Accounting Management is the single highest-search MBA specialisation in India. The Amity Online MBA in Finance and Accounting Management qualifies graduates for roles in corporate finance, financial planning and analysis, investment management, banking operations, and chartered accountancy support roles. The specialisation builds a rigorous grounding in financial statements, valuation models, treasury management, and regulatory compliance — subjects employers across BFSI, manufacturing, and infrastructure test at interview.

Amity University Online is NAAC A+ accredited and holds a NIRF #22 University rank for 2025. The Finance MBA carries the Amity University name on the final certificate, identical to the campus Finance MBA. UGC-DEB entitlement means the degree is valid for private sector hiring, government notifications where UGC-DEB degrees are accepted, and higher education admissions. EdifyEdu verifies these accreditation figures independently and does not receive commission from Amity.

The programme runs over 2 years across 4 semesters. Semesters 1 and 2 cover shared management core subjects. Semesters 3 and 4 cover the Finance and Accounting specialisation subjects. Live sessions are held on weekends; recorded lectures are available 24/7. See the main Amity MBA page at /universities/amity-university-online/mba for full programme overview.

syllabus.note: Sem 1-2 subjects are shared across all Amity MBA specialisations. See the main Amity MBA page for core subjects.
syllabus.emptyFallback:
Semesters 1 and 2 of the Amity Online MBA cover the management core: Accounting for Managers, Managerial Economics, Marketing Management, Statistics for Management, Professional Communication, Business Research Methods, Financial Management, Human Resource Management, Legal Aspects of Business, and Conflict Resolution and Management.

Semesters 3 and 4 cover the Finance and Accounting Management specialisation subjects. These include areas such as corporate finance, financial reporting, taxation, investment analysis, treasury and risk management, and financial modelling. The specific Sem 3 and Sem 4 subjects for Finance and Accounting Management are confirmed by Amity each admission cycle. Our counsellor can share the current spec-level syllabus within 24 hours. The final two semesters also include a minor project in Sem 3 and a major project in Sem 4 tied to your specialisation domain.

whoHires.body:
Finance and Accounting Management MBA graduates from Amity are hired across BFSI, manufacturing, infrastructure, and technology sectors. Entry-level roles (0-3 years experience) include: Financial Analyst, Junior Accountant, Accounts Executive, Audit Associate, and Banking Operations Executive. These roles are prevalent at Big Four accounting firms, mid-size NBFCs, and corporate finance teams at listed companies.

Mid-level roles (3-7 years) include: Finance Manager, FP&A Manager, Treasury Manager, Taxation Manager, and Corporate Controller. Senior finance professionals with this credential hold titles such as CFO, VP Finance, and General Manager Finance at large corporates and PSUs.

Key hiring sectors include: commercial banks (HDFC, ICICI, Axis, SBI), insurance companies (LIC, HDFC Life, ICICI Prudential), Big Four firms (Deloitte, PwC, EY, KPMG), NBFCs, government PSUs, and manufacturing conglomerates. The Amity Finance MBA is specifically recognised by employers who value the university's NIRF #22 ranking and QS World ranking, which signal research and academic standing relative to regional private universities.

skills.body:
The Finance and Accounting Management specialisation builds technical and analytical skills directly tested by BFSI employers. Core skills developed include:

Financial Statement Analysis: reading and interpreting balance sheets, P&L statements, and cash flow statements for corporate decision-making. Valuation Methods: DCF, comparables, asset-based valuation — used in investment banking, M&A, and private equity support functions. Cost and Management Accounting: standard costing, variance analysis, and activity-based costing relevant to manufacturing and service firms. Taxation and Compliance: direct tax, GST, and international taxation basics covering what most corporate finance roles require. Treasury and Risk Management: forex exposure, hedging instruments, and liquidity management for mid-to-large corporates. Financial Modelling: Excel-based models for budgeting, forecasting, and scenario analysis.

Soft skills developed include financial presentation to non-finance leadership, budget negotiation, and stakeholder communication. These are tested at CFO-track interviews and in FP&A roles at technology companies.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Amity Online MBA Finance against peers at similar fee points:

Amity vs LPU Finance MBA (/universities/lovely-professional-university-online/mba): LPU is NAAC A++ and NIRF #31. LPU Finance is marginally cheaper (around Rs 1,20,000). Amity's stronger QS World ranking and established placement network in Delhi NCR are differentiators for candidates targeting multinational hiring. LPU's stronger NAAC grade is useful for NIRF-gate government jobs.

Amity vs NMIMS Online MBA Finance (/universities/nmims-online/mba): NMIMS Online charges more (approximately Rs 2,50,000-3,00,000) and carries stronger brand recognition in Mumbai's financial services market. For candidates targeting Mumbai BFSI roles, NMIMS brand premium is real. For Delhi NCR or remote work, Amity Finance at Rs 1,99,000 offers better value per ranking point.

Amity vs Chandigarh University Finance MBA (/universities/chandigarh-university-online/mba): CU is NAAC A+ and NIRF #19. The fee is similar. CU has a stronger north India campus placement network but Amity has better brand recognition in corporate India across functions. Both are solid choices at this price tier.

fees.body: The total fee for the Amity University Online MBA (all specialisations including Finance and Accounting Management) is approximately Rs 1,99,000. This includes a one-time registration fee of Rs 2,500 and four semester fees of Rs 49,750 each. EMI options start from Rs 8,292 per month. Additional charges may apply for examination fees and material. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees each quarter. Always verify the exact current fee at the official Amity Online portal before making any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Amity University degree certificate carries the Amity University name and the MBA designation. The specialisation (Finance and Accounting Management) is noted on the marksheet and transcript. The certificate does not display the word online on the face, consistent with UGC-DEB norms. For a sample certificate confirming how the Finance specialisation is displayed, request one via /contact — our counsellor shares it within 24 hours.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from Amity Online MBA Finance alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Rohan Mehta
items.liked: Financial modeling depth and Amity brand recognition in BFSI interviews
items.disliked: Taxation content needs updating for GST 2.0 era
items.body: The Finance MBA from Amity completely changed how I read a balance sheet. Got hired at an NBFC as a Financial Analyst within 4 months of completion. Interviewers specifically mentioned Amity Finance brand. The valuation and FP&A modules are genuinely rigorous — not watered-down online content.

items.name: Priya Krishnamurthy
items.liked: Weekend schedule compatible with my CA articleship
items.disliked: Corporate law elective was too theoretical, needed more case studies
items.body: I was completing my CA articleship while doing this MBA. Weekend live sessions made it workable. The financial reporting and audit-adjacent content was genuinely useful for my articleship work. Degree helped me negotiate a better package when I switched to a Big Four after qualifying.

items.name: Saurabh Tiwari
items.liked: Faculty quality in treasury and risk management module
items.disliked: Placement portal could be more Finance-specific rather than generic MBA roles
items.body: Good programme overall. The treasury and forex risk management module was taught by a former banking professional and was excellent. I got a treasury analyst role at a listed manufacturing company. The Amity name opened the door — the content helped me crack the technical interview.

faqs.question: Can I switch from Finance and Accounting to another Amity MBA spec mid-programme?
faqs.answer: Specialisation changes are subject to Amity's academic policy and are typically not permitted after the first semester. Confirm the spec-change policy in writing with Amity admissions before enrolling. The spec is locked in at admission stage in most intakes.

faqs.question: What is the career outcome of Amity Finance MBA versus an Amity Marketing MBA?
faqs.answer: Finance MBA targets BFSI, corporate finance, and FP&A roles with higher starting salaries in banking. Marketing MBA targets brand, digital, and product roles with broader industry spread. Finance grads typically see a narrower but deeper industry entry; Marketing grads have wider sector access. Choose based on your target sector, not salary alone.

faqs.question: Does Amity Online Finance MBA have tie-ups with BFSI companies for placements?
faqs.answer: Amity University has a placement network of 400+ companies. Finance-specific recruiters include banks, NBFCs, and consulting firms. The online programme shares the campus placement network. However, placement support is assistance-based, not guaranteed. Candidates with 2+ years of work experience in Finance see significantly better outcomes.

faqs.question: What is the typical placement package for Amity Online MBA Finance graduates?
faqs.answer: Fresher Finance MBA graduates typically see offers in the Rs 4-7 lakh range. Working professionals with 3-5 years experience commonly see 20-40% salary jumps post-degree. Amity does not publish online MBA-specific placement statistics — ask for data on working professional outcomes at admissions stage.

faqs.question: Is prior Finance or Accounting experience required to enrol in this specialisation?
faqs.answer: No prior finance experience is required. The programme starts from fundamentals in Sem 1-2. However, candidates with prior finance work experience will get substantially more from the Sem 3-4 specialisation content. Freshers should pair this MBA with practical tools such as Excel financial modelling certifications to strengthen interview readiness.

chooseIf: You are targeting BFSI, corporate finance, FP&A, or investment support roles and want a NIRF-ranked university credential at under Rs 2 lakh.
You are in Delhi NCR or working remotely for a company that recognises Amity's brand in finance hiring.
You have 2+ years in any domain and want to pivot to finance with a recognised management degree.
consider: You are targeting Mumbai BFSI specifically — NMIMS Online Finance carries significantly stronger brand premium in that market despite higher fees.
You need NAAC A++ rather than A+ for specific government job notifications — consider LPU or CU Finance MBA instead.
You want an executive-format Finance MBA with stronger alumni network — Symbiosis Online or NMIMS may better suit senior professionals above 7 years experience.
